human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for administrative-support and business-services specificity, records-processing evidence quality, retention/access boundary handling, adjacent-lane routing, research contract coverage, functionality coverage, and validated completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to administrative-support and business-services records processing for client SOW, record package, record series, file-plan placement, metadata, attachment checklist, retention, access, QA, and audit evidence rather than generic office administration or adjacent legal, privacy, records, HR, finance, IAM, security, or official approval work.

Authority sources cover NAICS administrative support and business support services, BLS and O*NET clerical, file-clerk, and data-entry workflow references, NARA recordkeeping and electronic records requirements, ISO records-management principles, NIST security and privacy controls, and FTC disposal guidance.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal record package maintenance, missing attachments, unauthorized record changes, conflicting records, retention/privacy/access conflicts, official-record approval refusal, generic administrative overreach, adjacent-lane routing, realistic BPO terminology, missing system context, source conflicts, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/administrative-support-and-business-services/records-processing-specialist/ so records-processing work stays distinct from back-office processing, document-management system administration, records governance, privacy, legal, finance, HR, IAM, security, and approval lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.

Boundary enforcement remained strong around unauthorized changes, missing evidence, source conflicts, retention/access/privacy constraints, official-record authority, and overbroad administrative requests.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated records-processing specialist for administrative support and business-services record package completeness, governed metadata maintenance, retention/access escalation, and audit-trail discipline.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation results and readiness evidence.
